learning_outcomes.description: Know how to use a periodic table to combine atoms into molecules.
Know how to write and balance geochemical reactions.
Develop an understand of geochemical processes in surficial environments.
Apply the knowledge gained to real-life situations.
Working safely in a laboratory environment using geochemical reactions to design & implement chemical experiments.
Develop team building and teamwork skills during Group Work activities

assessments.category: Paper/ Report/ Annotation, Participation/ Student contribution, Practical/ Demonstration
assessments.task_description:
Students will use the guidance documents provided by the course instructor to write reports on the five laboratory projects:

  1. Bioremediation of an oil spill
  2. Bioleaching / Acid Mine Drainage
  3. Eutrophication
  4. Mineral Carbonation
  5. Chemical treatment of AMD

In this assessment, students will participate in hands-on, in-class group exercises designed to demonstrate the core concepts of the course. Although the activities are completed in groups, each student is required to prepare and submit an individual report. The assessment must be submitted via the Turnitin link before the assessment due date and time.

Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT)

Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Online submission via Turnitin only by the due date and time. Refer to Blackboard for the submission details. No hard copy or assignment cover sheets are required. Submission via email is not accepted.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
The in-semester exam is a closed-book exam that consists of multiple-choice and open questions covering the contents of Weeks 1 to 6. You will be provided with a copy of the Periodic Table for the examination.

Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT)

This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.

assessments.task_description:
This course requires regular attendance and active participation in weekly lectures and laboratory sessions.

A total of 10 laboratory practicals will be conducted from Week 1 to Week 10 in class. Students will be assessed based on their individual participation in class activities, as well as their contributions to weekly small-group laboratory work. Prior to each laboratory session, students must complete an in-person quiz designed to assess their planning, build on existing knowledge and understanding of safety requirements for that week’s practical activity. Each quiz is worth 1%.

The best 8 out of 10 quizzes will contribute to the final mark.

Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT)

This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: This assessment is conducted in class from Week 1 to Week 10. Students are required to attend their timetabled sessions to complete these assessments in class.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
